title: Latex beads migration assay test
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dfe7g","text":"The following protocol details how to test the migration of conjugated latex beads trough different nitrocellulose membranes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"5qdf3","text":"For preparation of sealed membranes we have used the protocol available in : dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8hdht26.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":77,"length":40}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"0DFDC686324D4909AA74F66421D7439C","url":"https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8hdht26"}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"1niku","text":"Cut the nitrocellulose  previously to th desired size of the strip. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"48jqf","text":"- Wax sealed nitrocellulose membranes ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bp34v","text":"- BSA 0.2 % in PBS Buffer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"78nv9","text":"- Conjugated latex beads Stocks at 1% wt in PBS-T (0.1 %) buffer.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"an6nb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7os5a","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare two FF170HP strips and three FF80HP strips of 1cm wide x 4 cm long. Wax print the microfluidic membranes following the protocol mentioned in the guideliness section.
Block one FF170HP and two FF80HP membranes by immersion in 0.2 % BSA solution in PBS. Let the membranes dry for 1h at room temperature, and let them on a dissecator at 4ºC overnight.

Aspire 20 µL of conjugated latex beads stock, and pipette them on the sample deposition area of the sealed membranes. It's recommendable placing the strips with a briefly inclination degree, avoiding the sample to fall down trough the membrane surface. (Placing the sample deposition region on the down region).
Wait 15 minutes until the liquid in the sample has migrated completely. Results can be directly visualized. Wait until the membranes have dried to manipulate them.
creator: Jorge Fernandez Mendez
